situation.headline: Active US-Iran kinetic exchange with strikes reaching Tehran vicinity and Iranian retaliation hitting US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan — the most significant direct US-Iran military confrontation in history is underway

domains.name: Middle East
domains.label: regional escalation 90d
domains.status: US-Iran conflict is kinetic and expanding: strikes on Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Tehran periphery, Engels-analogous Iranian air defense nodes, plus Iranian counter-strikes on US regional basing and Iraqi oil terminals signal a cascading multi-front war already in progress.

domains.name: Indo-Pacific
domains.label: China coercion escalation
domains.status: China's global favorability surging vs. US per fresh survey data while US military assets are consumed in the Gulf, creating a strategic opportunity window Beijing will assess carefully; no direct kinetic signal yet but conditions for opportunistic coercion are elevated.

domains.name: Europe
domains.label: NATO cohesion fracture
domains.status: Zelensky cabinet turmoil including Fedorov dismissal triggering street protests across five Ukrainian cities, a new prime minister installed, and ongoing Russian missile strikes on Kyiv strain Ukraine's political cohesion at a moment when US attention and resources are pivoting hard to the Gulf.

domains.name: Energy
domains.label: supply shock 60d
domains.status: Iran declaring Strait of Hormuz a red line, Houthi contingency plans to close Bab al-Mandeb, Iraq drone strikes suspending Basra crude loading, and a US-disabled tanker near Kharg Island constitute simultaneous threats to the three most critical global oil chokepoints — a supply shock is not a risk but an emergent reality.

domains.name: US Politics
domains.label: House flip Nov 2026
domains.status: Trump at 36% approval, White House leak probe, FCC gift scandal, and mounting domestic unrest including AI-related violence provide structural tailwinds for Democratic gains, though wartime rally effects and a fractured Democratic field — AOC leads at 16%, Newsom at 20%, no dominant figure — create uncertainty; Kalshi shows no dominant 2028 Dem frontrunner.

situation.unseen: The simultaneous interdiction pressure on all three of the world's critical energy chokepoints — Hormuz, Bab al-Mandeb, and the Iraqi terminal complex — is not being read as a coordinated Iranian-Houthi operational plan to strangle global energy supply rather than simply retaliate militarily; if Iran commands Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure concurrent with Hormuz mining threats and Iraqi terminal drone campaigns, the global economic shock could dwarf the military exchange itself, and China — holding massive dollar reserves and energy stockpiles — would be uniquely positioned to exploit the resulting Western financial stress.
situation.criticalWindow: The next 30-72 hours: whether Iran executes on its threat to target regional infrastructure of all US partner nations in response to strikes near Tehran will determine if this remains a US-Iran bilateral exchange or metastasizes into a GCC-wide war; simultaneously, whether Kuwait and Bahrain invoke Article 5-equivalent mutual defense triggers with the US will lock in coalition commitments and force congressional war authorization questions that the White House leak probe and 36% approval rating make politically toxic.
watchFor: Iranian mining operations or physical closure attempts in the Strait of Hormuz — the declared red line threshold that would trigger oil price dislocation above any prior historical precedent
Houthi formal announcement of Bab al-Mandeb closure in coordination with Tehran, potentially choking 12-15% of global trade simultaneously with Hormuz pressure
Chinese PLA naval or air activity near Taiwan or in the South China Sea exploiting US carrier and CENTCOM asset concentration in the Gulf

Todd Blanche AG confirmation surge — Kalshi contract spiking 14 cents to 87% in 24 hours signals imminent nomination announcement, which combined with the White House phone seizure leak probe suggests a significant domestic political-legal maneuver is underway concurrent with the military crisis
situation.confidenceNote: Critical uncertainties include: actual damage assessment from US strikes on Iranian military capacity is unknown from OSINT alone; Iranian retaliation claims against US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan are Iranian-sourced and may be exaggerated for domestic consumption; the IRGC MQ-9 shootdown claim is unconfirmed by CENTCOM; Zelensky cabinet dynamics and whether the new Ukrainian PM signals a negotiating posture shift is opaque; Trump's 111-missile claim against USS Abraham Lincoln is unverified and potentially inflated for domestic political effect; the Kalshi AG contract move is the clearest high-confidence signal in the dataset.
situation.topPattern: The US is simultaneously prosecuting a kinetic campaign against Iran while experiencing a domestic political crisis at 36% approval with an internal leak probe — the Kalshi Todd Blanche AG contract jumping 14 cents to 87% in a single session suggests the administration is moving to consolidate DOJ control precisely as it conducts unauthorized-feeling military operations, creating a constitutional stress scenario where the legal architecture of the war, the domestic political survival of the presidency, and the physical security of global energy supply are all peaking in crisis concurrently with no clear off-ramp visible in any theater.

signals.headline: US strikes Iranian command centers, air defenses near Strait of Hormuz; Iran retaliates with drone/missile claims against Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan
signals.summary: US executed sustained strike campaign against Iranian military infrastructure including Bandar Abbas and Greater Tunb Island. Iran's Armed Forces claim reciprocal strikes on regional US assets. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz a red line and warned of infrastructure targeting if US strikes Iranian power/bridges. Houthis reportedly preparing to close Bab al-Mandeb in coordination with Iran.
signals.signal: Direct US-Iran military escalation now active with stated redlines on critical chokepoints. Risk of cascading closure of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandeb would disrupt 20% of global oil transit and crater energy markets. Coordination between Iran, Houthis, and potential Iraqi actors suggests multi-vector blockade planning. Market has not yet repriced oil/shipping derivatives for sustained conflict scenario.
signals.contradicts: Assumption that Trump administration would avoid kinetic Iran conflict; instead shows direct strike campaign with no diplomatic off-ramp visible.

signals.headline: Ukraine cabinet reshuffle amid military procurement disputes; new PM appointed as Defense Minister resignation sparks protests
signals.summary: Zelensky forced Defense Minister Fedorov to resign amid reports linking dismissal to military contracting decisions. Protests erupted in five major cities. Zelensky appointed Sergii Koretskyi as new PM while promising Fedorov role TBD. Zelensky acknowledges systemic defense/military sector problems requiring decisive resolution.
signals.signal: Leadership instability in Ukraine during peak conflict suggests possible diversion of resources from frontline to internal power consolidation. Corruption allegations in defense procurement could degrade military capability. Timing of cabinet purge while Russia conducts sustained ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv (6th this month) signals possible loss of initiative or morale crisis.
signals.contradicts: Narrative of Ukrainian unified war effort; internal faction disputes over contracts/personnel now overshadowing military operations.

signals.headline: Trump approval at 36%; prediction markets show Todd Blanche 87% for next AG; no viable Democratic 2028 challenger above 20%
signals.summary: Presidential approval underwater at 36%. Todd Blanche AG odds consolidated at 87% with 14-cent 24h gain, signaling near-certainty. Gavin Newsom (20%) and AOC (16%) lead fragmented Democratic nominee field; Harris/Buttigieg under 10%. JD Vance 40% for GOP 2028 nominee; Ron DeSantis only 5-6%.
signals.signal: Market-priced expectation of Blanche confirmation signals Trump intent to install loyalist AG without resistance. Fractured Democratic primary field suggests no coherent 2028 opposition coalition forming. Approval floor at 36% with White House in active leak probe (phones seized) and DHS crisis (arrests/shootings) indicates erosion of legitimacy but not near-term electoral threat.
signals.contradicts: Claims of institutional resistance or Senate obstruction to Trump personnel choices; markets pricing smooth AG confirmation.

signals.headline: AI backlash turns violent; executives report fear for lives; China bans AI companion emotional dependency via new laws
signals.summary: Multiple sources report violent escalation against AI sector leaders. China enacted new laws explicitly targeting emotional dependency on AI companions following user farewell posts. Suggests coordinated regulatory crackdown on synthetic intimacy technologies alongside grassroots hostility.
signals.signal: Emerging bifurcation of AI regulation: US political/media backlash emphasizing corporate accountability; China targeting consumer AI psychology as social threat. Violence against executives may trigger security-focused retreat from AI development or insurance/liability spiral. Chinese move signals concern about AI replacing human social bonds as threat to state control.
signals.contradicts: Silicon Valley framing of AI as inevitable/beneficial; now facing both state-directed regulation and vigilante resistance.

signals.headline: Trump administration imposes 25% tariff on Brazilian imports over Lula 'bad faith' negotiations; Mexico rejects cartel-government linkage claims
signals.summary: USTR directed to impose 25% tariff on Brazil following Trump judgment that Lula failed good-faith negotiation. Mexico's Sheinbaum publicly rejects US allegations of government cartel ties. Reflects escalating Trump trade/security pressure on largest Latin American economies.
signals.signal: Trump weaponizing tariffs against middle-power allies (Brazil, Mexico) for non-compliance on Trump-defined terms. Brazil tariff suggests no exemption for BRICS partners or regional economic powers. Mexico tariff threat over 'cartel links' signals Trump may use security justifications for protectionist measures. Risk of Latin American counter-alignment with China/Russia and USMCA destabilization.
signals.contradicts: Post-NAFTA consensus of hemispheric integration; instead shows unilateral tariff deployment against regional partners.

signals.headline: Iraq suspends crude loading after drone strike on Basra terminal; Ukrainian USVs disable Russian shadow fleet tankers in Black Sea
signals.summary: Iraqi authorities halted all terminal loading following drone strike attributed to Iranian proxies on tanker at Basra Oil Terminal. Ukraine struck two Russian shadow fleet tankers in eastern Black Sea, disabling both. Ongoing attacks in southern Iraq suggest escalating energy infrastructure targeting.
signals.signal: Iran-proxy attack on Iraqi oil infrastructure raises cost of Middle East energy production/transport during active US-Iran strike campaign. Ukraine's targeting of Russian shadow fleet indicates deliberate disruption of sanctions evasion logistics. Combined effect: reduction in global crude supply (Iraq production offline, Russian circumvention shipping hit, Hormuz closure risk imminent). Oil market under-hedged for supply destruction scenario.
signals.contradicts: Assumption that sanctions and proxy conflicts remain contained to political/military domains; now directly targeting energy production/logistics.

scanNote: OSINT feeds show simultaneous kinetic escalation in three theaters (Middle East, Ukraine, Black Sea), US political weakness (36% approval, leak probe), and emerging great-power competition on AI/trade; signal quality degraded by Drudge headline sensationalism but PIZZINT OSINT and market pricing consistent.

markets.label: Zelensky-Putin meeting before Jan 2029
markets.rationale: Kalshi already dropped 17 cents in 24h; ongoing Russian missile strikes on Kyiv, Ukrainian internal political turmoil with cabinet reshuffles and street protests, and no ceasefire framework visible makes a direct leaders' meeting in this timeframe very unlikely.

markets.label: Todd Blanche as next AG
markets.rationale: Market surged 14 cents to 87% in 24 hours, signaling strong informed-money conviction; the ongoing White House leak probe and phone-seizure reporting suggest internal legal/political pressure that would accelerate an AG appointment, and Blanche's existing Trump legal relationship makes him the clear frontrunner.

markets.label: AOC as 2028 Dem nominee
markets.rationale: Trump sitting at 36% approval creates an opening for a bold insurgent progressive, and AOC leads the crowded Dem nominee field at 16% on Kalshi, but her ideological position and electability concerns in a general election keep her actual probability below market pricing.

markets.label: Gavin Newsom as 2028 Dem nominee
markets.rationale: At 20% market price, Newsom remains structurally well-positioned as the most organized non-incumbency Dem prospect, but California brand liabilities and a fractured party field with Ossoff and AOC gaining traction temper his actual odds.

markets.label: Jon Ossoff as 2028 Dem nominee
markets.rationale: Ossoff has seen incremental Kalshi gains to 15% with above-average volume, likely reflecting his moderate Southern profile as an antidote to Trump in swing states, but he lacks national infrastructure and name recognition to justify a higher probability at this stage.

markets.label: Marco Rubio wins 2028 presidency
markets.rationale: Rubio is positioned as a credible heir apparent at 17% win probability on Kalshi, but the 25% Brazil tariff announcement he fronted and the Iran crisis management burden may create political liabilities; with Vance at 40% for the Republican nomination Rubio's path is narrow.

markets.label: J.D. Vance as 2028 GOP nominee
markets.rationale: Vance leads the Republican nomination market at 40% with stable open interest; his incumbency-adjacent positioning as VP during an active wartime presidency is structurally advantageous, though Trump's 36% approval and the Iran escalation carry downside risk for the entire Republican brand heading into 2028.

watchSignals: Iran-US military escalation cycle: Watch for next Iranian retaliatory strike window (72-168 hours). IRGC rhetoric on 'red lines' and Strait of Hormuz closure plans suggest imminent coordinated action with Houthis/proxies. Monitor strait transit disruption and oil price response.

Ukraine defense ministry instability cascade: Fedorov removal triggered street protests in five major cities and Zelensky admission of 'problems in defense sector.' Watch for additional cabinet removals, parliamentary confidence votes, and morale impact on military command during active Russian offensive.

US-Brazil trade escalation: Trump's 25% tariff directive on Brazilian imports signals willingness to use economic coercion on left-leaning regional governments. Monitor Lula administration response, MERCOSUR fracturing, and broader LatAm alignment shifts away from US.

Domestic US institutional stress: Concurrent signals of White House phone seizures (leak probe), NYT fighting subpoenas, DHS crisis response, and AI violence backlash suggest widening gap between executive branch and press/judiciary. Watch for additional media legal challenges and personnel purges.

2028 Democratic nominee market crystallization: AOC (16%), Newsom (20%), and Ossoff (15%) showing micro-traction in prediction markets while Harris (8%) and Buttigieg (5%) fade. Watch for early primary signals, donor network formation, and whether establishment coalesces around one moderate alternative.

Russian Black Sea shadow fleet targeting: Ukrainian USVs destroying tanker assets in denial-of-service pattern. Monitor whether Russia rotates remaining shadow fleet assets, escalates naval countermeasures, or accelerates Arctic route exploration to circumvent Black Sea blockade.

groundTruth.headline: US Central Command conducted sustained multi-wave strike campaign on Iranian military infrastructure including command centers, air defenses, missile/drone sites, and coastal surveillance facilities near Strait of Hormuz, Bandar Abbas, and Greater Tunb Island
groundTruth.summary: US strikes represent major escalation beyond previous red-line warnings. Iran claims retaliatory attacks on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, and downed US MQ-9 Reaper. Multiple rounds of active air defense engagement reported across Gulf states. Strait of Hormuz now actively contested military zone.
groundTruth.signal: Risk of sustained tit-for-tat cycle exceeding Cold War patterns; potential for unintended escalation causing regional economic disruption (oil markets, global trade). US military posture indicates readiness for extended campaign, not single-strike deterrence.

groundTruth.headline: Ukraine President Zelensky dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov amid ongoing Russian offensive; Zelensky publicly admitted responsibility for unresolved 'problems in defense and military sector' requiring 'decisive' action
groundTruth.summary: Fedorov removal triggered spontaneous protests in Kyiv, Kharkiv, Lviv, Dnipro, Odesa. Zelensky appointed new Prime Minister Sergii Koretskyi. Multiple reports suggest contractor/procurement disputes preceded firing, raising questions about wartime governance effectiveness.
groundTruth.signal: Command instability during active combat operations increases force degradation risk. Domestic political friction over defense procurement may signal either legitimate corruption crackdown or destabilizing power struggle. Watch for military morale/AWOL patterns and Russian operational tempo exploitation.

groundTruth.domain: US Domestic - Executive Branch Institutional Friction
groundTruth.headline: White House officials ordered to turn over phones in intensifying leak probe; New York Times filing motion to quash administration subpoenas; DHS facing 'crisis moment' with mounting arrests and fatal shootings
groundTruth.summary: Simultaneous signals of internal security investigations (phone seizures), media litigation resistance, and law enforcement operational crises. FCC officials also revealed taking corporate gifts during regulatory approval process (Paramount). Pattern suggests breakdown in institutional trust mechanisms.
groundTruth.signal: Escalating confrontation between executive and press/judiciary reduces governance transparency. Internal security apparatus activation may indicate genuine breach or overreach perception. Predicts further legal challenges to executive orders and potential institutional paralysis on secondary priorities.

groundTruth.headline: Trump administration directed 25% tariff on Brazilian imports, citing President Lula's failure to negotiate 'in good faith'; Mexico government rejecting US cartel-linkage claims
groundTruth.summary: Tariff represents unilateral economic punishment of friendly elected government. Mexico's public rejection signals resistance to US pressure narrative. Coincides with Panama Canal sovereignty discussions and Mexico Interoceanic Corridor positioning as US alternative.
groundTruth.signal: Regional realignment away from US economic dominance. Brazil and Mexico exploring alternative trade partnerships and infrastructure independence. Likely triggers MERCOSUR/USMCA negotiation crises and Chinese/Indian investment acceleration in region.

groundTruth.headline: Survey data shows China has become more popular than USA in much of world; Trump approval at 36% domestically
groundTruth.summary: Soft power metric indicates multi-year erosion of US cultural/diplomatic influence. Combined with 36% domestic approval rating and high-profile AI executive safety concerns ('fear for lives'), suggests simultaneous internal and external legitimacy challenges.
groundTruth.signal: Long-term strategic position weakening. Allies reconsidering alignment. Domestic political instability reduces US credibility on international commitments. Creates opening for Chinese/Russian/Indian expansion in previously US-aligned regions.
